Distribution of funds for project activities.
(Lysis code 4702A019)
Government of Maharashtra
Soil and Water Conservation Department
Government Decision No: Rev. 2026 / Q. 128 / Jal-4
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400032.
Date: March 31, 2026.

Read: - 1) Department of Finance, Government Decision No. Finance-2025 / Q. 44 / Earth-3, dated 7th April, 2025, 29th January, 2026 and 12th March, 2026 2) Regional Water Conservation Officer, Soil and Water Conservation Department, Thane, Pune, Nashik, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Amravati and Nagpur Office Total 84 proposals mentioned in Form-A.
3) Government Decision No: Asank-2026 / P.No.27 / Jal-11,
Dated March 31, 2026

Proposal: Demand for works for new / on-going minor irrigation (water harvesting) projects of 0 to 100 hectares irrigation potential in non-tribal areas No. ZH-5, Account Head 4702 A019 was budgeted at ₹ID1 crore in the year 2025-26. However, due to a large reduction in the revised estimate of the year 2025-26 and the relatively large demand for funds being proposed, a proposal was submitted for approval of the Finance Department to make available savings under the scheme by way of redeployment under other schemes.

In accordance with the above, Reference No. 3 Orders have been issued here. The following decision has been taken regarding the utilization of funds made available to the Department pursuant to this order.

Government Decision: Payment of pending payments of eligible and completed works received with the Government for new / under-progress projects of 0 to 100 hectares irrigation capacity in the respective proposals of Regional Offices of Thane, Pune, Nashik, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Amravati and Nagpur.
Reference No. Total Rs. 5325.70 Lakhs (Rs.
Fifty-three crore twenty-five lakh seventy thousand only) attached to the fund "Form-A"
are being distributed.
The aforesaid funds are to be distributed subject to the following general conditions and conditions:
It is: -
a) Water Conservation Corporation, District Planning for the works of the projects in the said proposals.
Distributed by the committee or other source, if funds are available.
The funds being made should not be withdrawn or distributed,
b) Administrative approval as well as revised administrative approval as approved by the Government.
Funds should not be spent in excess of the cost.
a) If the projects in these proposals do not require funding, the funds
Under no circumstances should the mutual be distributed to another project,
d) If for any reason, the fund remains unfilled, the fund is immediately transferred to the Government.
to be returned,
e) Guidelines in the Reference Circular of the Department of Finance regarding the disbursement of funds.
Follow strictly.
f) Ensure the quality of the work, make payments, and report compliance.
submitted to the government.
3. The amount disbursed by it pertains to regional water conservation.
The officer is drawn by the Soil and Water Conservation Department from the respective districts under their jurisdiction.
The water should be distributed to the conservation authorities expeditiously.
4 The expenditure on this should be sub-scribed to the next head of account and
2025-26 to be subtracted from the approved provision for this financial year.

The Water Conservation Officer, Department of Soil and Water Conservation shall be the Additional Commissioner concerned.

will remain.
